what_it_does: Synthesizes seven parallel explorer/research waves into one navigable, build-driving document for a fresh instrument-surface frontend, cataloguing what to reuse, avoid, and constrain from the existing backend, data stores, geometric seed, and proposed design language.
observations: Companion to MANDATE.md and the second of three loop-prep documents.
Distinguishes code-grounded findings (file:line citations) from research-grounded 2026 design proposals.
§A identifies the address-on-UI + context-attach mechanism as the single permitted UI inheritance.
§B catalogues 61+ bridge endpoints across categories: projection, stream, context, cognition, content/graphs, operator surface, voice/chat, graph mutation, walkthrough/trial.
§C inventories real store counts: 3,038 embedded corpus units, 6,120 events, 72 graphs, 2,793 vectors, 18,119 ref versions, 29 roles, 8 projections, etc.
§D lists 12 lens groups (G2–G12) and seed geometric proportions: dyadic grid m=2^d, m/2 rings, even angular division, normalized radius band [0.06,1.0], resonance law 2π/n=(1/n)^k.
§E proposes a fresh warm-paper design token set: ground neutrals, near-black ink, 5 muted pigments, Fluent-style elevation, 1.25 modular type scale, dyadic spacing.
§F recommends SVG+Canvas hybrid rendering with Framer Motion / Motion as the single motion system, no-teleport, layoutId paired with ui:// address.
§G specifies three authored form factors (desktop 1440×900, portrait 390×844, landscape 844×390) and a five-state disclosure grammar: resting→hovered→opened→pinned→dismissed.

§H lists nine corrections/tightenings to the MANDATE/Implementation Guide, including corpus count update, L15 promotion seam naming, L14 embedding task, substrate grounding, and SSE line-cite conflict.
Many items flagged as OPEN and requiring Tim to decide by reacting to renders.
Recurring constraints: fail-loud, registry-is-truth, no hardcodes, 4,000-char R2 budget, no teleporting, safe-area handling, touch-target sizing.
standouts: The address-on-UI + context-attach mechanism is the sole inheritance from the old canvas; everything else is disposable.
/api/projection is the keystone pure-read endpoint; the surface is thin over a thick contract.
Real-data inventory proves the build must connect to non-toy data (3,038 units, 6,120 events, 2,793 vectors).
Seed geometric proportions are treated as the actual UI layout law, not just data visualization.
Every interactive element must carry both a ui:// address and a layoutId, coupling addressing and motion.
Three form factors are authored independently, not responsive-degraded.
conventions: Sections prefixed with § and a letter for navigation.
Each section answers: what was explored, what was found, what it means for the build.
Findings tagged as REUSE / AVOID / CONSTRAINT / OPEN.
Code-grounded claims cite file:line; research-grounded claims cite 2026 design sources.
Inferences are explicitly marked; fail-loud behavior is emphasized.
Registry-is-truth: no hardcoded sector counts, binding ids, pole vectors, route lists.
No ranking language; neutral, file-local synthesis.
concerns: File is truncated at the end of §H; §I and any following content are missing.
Some data counts are inferred from the MANDATE rather than independently re-counted.
SSE handler line-cite conflict between explorers is unresolved.
Query-string parsing inconsistency noted as a latent integration risk.
§E design tokens are explicitly proposals, not settled, requiring render reaction.
A large number of OPEN items may block freezing implementation criteria.
Dense cross-references may be hard to action without a Wave-2 read of projection.py return shapes and address_help structure.
notes: This is the build-prep research synthesis that turns parallel explorer findings into concrete reuse/avoid/constraints for the new instrument surface, with the address mechanism as the only permitted UI inheritance.
questions: What is the complete per-point field list returned by projection.py's project()?
What is the exact address_help response structure from Suite.address_help?
Which second axis will serve as the spine alongside time for commensurate scaling?
Does the surface need a Canvas layer for chrome, or only for the ~3,038-point cloud?
What are the verified Motion spring defaults after checking the source?
Should surfaced/ show all 92 items or latest-per-kind?
Are the 3 scale records per-graph or global?
How deep is addresses.json (tens vs hundreds of addresses)?
Does a generic 'promote candidate → proposal' verb exist, or must it be built atop propose_*?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it presents itself as a synthesis of explorer waves into a build-driving document and does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Addressability as the relational spine of the UI
Progressive disclosure and near-empty resting states
Seed geometry as the UI layout grid
One shared store, no data silos
intention_signals: To give engineering a grounded, constraint-rich starting point for building the new surface while preserving only the mandated address/context substrate and aligning every decision with the MANDATE.
novelty: It treats Tim's geometric seed proportions as the actual UI layout grid and mandates that every interactive DOM node carry both a ui:// address and a motion layoutId, fusing the relational spine with no-teleport animation.
contribution: A structured bridge between explorer findings and implementation, cataloguing endpoints, data counts, geometric lenses, design proposals, rendering substrate, layout specs, and explicit corrections/open questions so the build can start from verified substrate rather than assumptions.
summary_for_embedding: A build-prep research synthesis document that maps the existing address/context mechanism, bridge API catalogue, real data inventory, geometric lens catalogue, proposed design tokens, rendering substrate, and layout specs into concrete reuse/avoid/constraints for a fresh instrument surface.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate parallel explorer findings into one actionable, build-driving reference that preserves the mandated address substrate and guides the new surface implementation.
